ation links History Computer-aided design of integrated circuits has been an area of excellence at University of California at Berkeley since Donald O. Pederson's foundational work on Spice in 1972. Since that time Berkeley faculty have been recipi

Since that time Berkeley faculty have been recipients of many top honors in this area, including: Six of the twenty three Kaufman awards given by the Electronic Design Automation Consortium Eight Best Paper Awards at the Design Automation Conference

t Paper Awards at the Design Automation Conference Best Paper Awards from the journal Transactions on Computer-Aided Design Four papers in "The Best Papers in Twenty-Five Years of the Design Automation Conference" Seven papers in "The Be

ign Automation Conference" Seven papers in "The Best of (Twenty-Years) of the International Conference on Computer-Aided Design " Forty books and over 2,000 papers that have collected over 20,000 citations as measured by the Scientific Li

20,000 citations as measured by the Scientific Literature Digital Library Numerous other prestigious awards including two Darlington Awards ,a Guillemin-Cauer Award, and a ISSCC Jack Raper Award Three faculty are members of the National Academy of E

e faculty are members of the National Academy of Engineering All faculty are Fellows of the IEEE Industrial and Academic Impact Berkeley's impact can hardly be measured by awards alone. Berkeley has had significant impact on both academia and indu

had significant impact on both academia and industry in a number of ways: Berkeley Ph. D's and post-doctoral fellows populate the world's top academic institutions. Our graduates and post-docs include: Associate Professor Adnan Aziz ,

include: Associate Professor Adnan Aziz , University of Texas at Austin Associate Professor Shuvra S. Bhattacharyya , University of Maryland Assistant Professor Luca Carloni , Columbia University Professor Kwang

, Columbia University Professor Kwang-Ting (Tim) Cheng , University of California, Santa Barbara Assistant Professor Luca Daniel , Massachusetts Institute of Technology Professor Luca de Alfaro , Unive

ology Professor Luca de Alfaro , University of California, Santa Cruz Professor Srinivas Devadas , Massachusetts Institute of Technology Assistant Professor Stephen Edwards , Columbia University Associate Professor

sor Thomas M. Parks , Colgate University Professor Miodrag Potkonjak , University of California, Los Angeles Senior Lecturer John Reekie , University of Technology Sydney Assistant Professor Dennis Sylvester ,

y Sydney Assistant Professor Dennis Sylvester , University of Michigan Professor Jacob White , Massachusetts Institute of Technology Berkeley faculty have played key roles in founding two of the largest electronic design aut

founding two of the largest electronic design automation companies: Cadence Design Systems Synopsys Graduates have also founded many successful electronic design automation companies: Dr. Ewald Detjens founded Exemplar Logic, which became a

ald Detjens founded Exemplar Logic, which became a wholly-owned subsidiary of Mentor Graphics Corporation Dr. Ramin Hojati is the President and founder of Averant Dr. Yuji Kukimoto is a co-founder of Extreme DA Dr. Alex Saldanha and Dr. Rick McGeer

Extreme DA Dr. Alex Saldanha and Dr. Rick McGeer founded Softface, Inc., which has been acquired by Ariba, Inc. Professor Res Saleh is a founder and former Chairman of Simplex Solutions, which has been acquired by Cadence Desi

hich has been acquired by Cadence Design Systems Dr. Hamid Savoj co-founded Magma Design Automation and is serving as Senior Vice President, Product Development Dr. Vigyan Singhal was the founding CEO of Jasper Design Automation and is cu

founding CEO of Jasper Design Automation and is currently Chief Technology Officer Research software produced at Berkeley has been a key influence in the development of many commercial products: MIS influenced the creation of the Synopsis Design C

S influenced the creation of the Synopsis Design Compiler Polis led to the development of Cadence VCC Ptolemy Classic influenced Synopsys System Studio and Agilent Advanced Development System Spectre and RELAX produced the Cadence Spectre Circuit Sim

and RELAX produced the Cadence Spectre Circuit Simulator and Spectre-RF Simulation tools Spice has influenced HSPICE and many other related products Berkeley faculty continue to play key roles as founders, directors, investors, and advisors to a

founders, directors, investors, and advisors to a number of important companies in the electronic design automation and semiconductor industries: Cadence Design Systems Mentor Graphics Corporation Synopsys Stretch Inc. Tensilica as well as many n
